notes: These files are designed for rendering detailed type information, not just range checking.
Fields are intentionally verbose so the UI can choose what to show.
mainstreamSizes describes common ABIs/platforms; standardGuarantee describes what the language standard itself promises.
languageNotes: JavaScript has only two built-in numeric language types: Number and BigInt.
Many names like int32/uint32/float32 are not independent language variable types; they are conversion/storage models used by abstract operations and typed arrays.

types.definition: IEEE 754 binary64 floating-point numeric type used for nearly all ordinary JavaScript numbers.
types.summary: Default and historically dominant numeric type in JavaScript. Integers and fractions both live here until you intentionally use BigInt or binary storage APIs.
types.standardGuarantee: ECMAScript defines Number as one of the two numeric types; mainstream behavior is IEEE 754 binary64 with NaN, infinities, and signed zero.
types.precision: 53 bits of integer precision; safe integer range is -(2^53-1)..(2^53-1).
types.rounding: Ordinary arithmetic follows IEEE 754 binary64 rounding behavior. Many decimal fractions are inexact.
specialRules: Bitwise operators first convert via ToInt32 / ToUint32 paths.
NaN is not equal to itself; use Number.isNaN.
Large integers beyond safe range lose unit precision.

types.definition: Arbitrary-precision integer numeric type distinct from Number.
types.summary: Use for exact integer arithmetic beyond safe Number range or when fractional values are not allowed by the domain.
types.standardGuarantee: ECMAScript defines BigInt as the second built-in numeric type. Precision is conceptually unbounded except by resources.
specialRules: Cannot be mixed implicitly with Number in arithmetic; explicit conversion is required.
Division truncates toward zero because the result must remain integral.
No NaN or Infinity values exist in BigInt.

types.definition: 32-bit signed integer model produced by ECMAScript abstract operation ToInt32 and by Int32Array storage semantics.
types.summary: Not a separate variable declaration type in the language. Useful to document how bitwise operators and typed arrays reinterpret/convert values.
types.standardGuarantee: Value is modulo 2^32 then interpreted as signed two's-complement 32-bit result.
specialRules: Fractional part is truncated toward zero before wrapping model is applied in the abstract operation path.
Very large Numbers first pass through finite-number semantics and then 32-bit reduction.

types.definition: 32-bit unsigned integer model produced by ToUint32 and Uint32Array storage.
types.summary: Again, not an independent declaration type; it is the model behind many binary and bitwise operations.
specialRules: Useful for forcing unsigned interpretation of bitwise results.

types.definition: 8-bit signed integer element type for Int8Array/DataView-style storage.
types.summary: Binary storage view, not standalone language declaration type.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly 8-bit signed two's-complement storage semantics in typed-array memory model.
specialRules: Writes wrap/narrow through the typed-array conversion semantics.

types.definition: 8-bit unsigned integer element type for Uint8Array/DataView-style storage.
types.summary: Canonical raw byte array storage model in JavaScript.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly 8-bit unsigned storage semantics in typed arrays.
specialRules: Wraps modulo 256 on assignment through storage semantics.

types.definition: 8-bit unsigned storage model that clamps to 0..255 instead of pure modulo wrap.
types.summary: Mostly associated with image/pixel APIs. Very important because it rounds/clamps differently from ordinary Uint8.
types.standardGuarantee: Assignment uses clamping semantics to the byte range, not plain wraparound.
specialRules: Fractional values are rounded/clamped according to the specified Uint8Clamped conversion semantics, not simply truncated modulo 256.

types.definition: IEEE 754 binary32 storage element in typed arrays / DataView.
types.summary: Useful when JS code intentionally stores low-level 32-bit floats for graphics, WASM interop, DSP, or compact binary buffers.
types.standardGuarantee: Binary32 storage semantics in typed arrays.
types.rounding: Writes round to binary32 representable values.
specialRules: Not a separate language variable type; ordinary numeric expressions still use Number unless stored/read through these APIs.

types.definition: IEEE 754 binary64 storage element in typed arrays / DataView.
types.summary: Closest binary buffer analogue to ordinary Number storage, but still a memory view type rather than a separate declaration type.

types.definition: Signed 64-bit integer storage element for BigInt typed arrays.
types.summary: Binary storage for 64-bit signed integer lanes backed by BigInt values in JS.
types.standardGuarantee: Modulo 2^64 storage interpreted as signed two's-complement 64-bit values.

types.definition: Unsigned 64-bit integer storage element for BigUint64Array.
types.summary: Binary storage for exact 64-bit unsigned values in JS using BigInt.
